name: The Ravenous Pig
address: 565 W Fairbanks Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789, USA

reviews.text: Food was very good. The duck beat the one I had in Paris. The contrast and flavors were all there, umami flavor, break was a little sweet and the saltiness of the duck plus the richness of the barolo all in one dish. The steak was nice, it does come with a lot of fries but they taste so good that I didn’t mind. Our server was super nice. Overall a beautiful night very quiet and calm. Will be back

The menu was creative! The ambiance was great with different vibes in different sections/areas. Service was awesome!

Only drawback in the goodness had was slightly too oily. Otherwise it was great!

We had the Mushroom & Guyure Bignets for starters. Where great. Inside was a bit more moist than expected, but nevertheless enjoyable. For inside was the fried eggplant. Not sure if olive oil or truffle oil was drizzled on top or the oil from frying settled on the plate when served, but that’s my main issue. For the main  we had the Steak Frites and Iberico Durocher Pork Tenderloin. Delicious food!

For years I had wanted to try The Ravenous Pig in Winter Park, Florida. I went last night and had a great experience! The decor is at the same time functional, elegant and modern. I sat at the bar (which I love to do at most restaurants for faster friendlier service and conversations). I ordered an Albariño white wine from their extensive single glass wine list. The menu is vast and varied, focusing on American fare with either a Southern or European twist. I decided to have two appetizers and one of their vegetable dishes: creamed mushrooms and escargots(snails), roasted marrow bones with toast and onion marmelade, and a fried eggplant dish. I found the roasted bone marrow delicious, like the best butter and marmalade, ever. The mushroom and escargots dish was very creamy but needed a touch of acid and salt, and the eggplant dish was a little bit greasy, but had good flavor. The dessert I had was banana ice cream with chocolate ganache. The service was very attentive and friendly.

Had the 20oz wagyu piranha. This was delicious, however the size of steak and amount of fat was a little too much for me. However, this is how it is served.  So just in case you dont like fat on steak. I dont recommend. Otherwise, recommend this steak!  I also ordered the potatoes and mushrooms side.  The potatoes melted in mouth.  The roasted trumpet mushrooms were also tasty. They are bold and strong in flavor.  My wife had the chicken marsala and finished the entire plate.  Well done Ravenous Pig!  Great food and service!  Make sure to try the working man porter.  Delicious also!!!

When a Legend Forgets to Turn Up the Heat

Let's be clear: The Ravenous Pig is an OG of the Orlando food scene, a place that has earned every ounce of its formidable reputation. Walking in feels like visiting culinary royalty, and you rightly expect a feast for the ages. The room hums with a comfortable energy and the service is solid—everything is in place for a stellar meal.
But even royalty can have an off day.

The menu reads like a list of future classics, yet what arrived on the plate felt more like a pleasant memory of a better dish. Our main courses were served at a temperature so timid, you’d think they were afraid to make a scene. The flavors were all there, whispering their potential, but they were hopelessly muted by that distinct lack of heat.
It's the kind of slip that suggests a kitchen running on autopilot; a restaurant so confident in its own legend that it has forgotten the small, fiery details that forged it in the first place.

The feeling wasn't disappointment, exactly, but more like nostalgia for a spark we had just missed.

We left feeling like we'd heard a great band's perfectly fine B-side. It was good, for sure, but we came for the chart-topping single. Here's hoping The Ravenous Pig finds its sizzle again soon—we're ready for an encore.

reviews.text: Natalia was an amazing bartender... I can not stress this enough. Super attentive, friendly and exceptional customer service. The manager Roxy super friendly and attentive. No complaints when looking for a fun Monday preview of your weekend with good food, great drinks and calm atmosphere definitely check out Mamazzita in Orlando.

Beautiful space, disappointing experience

We visited this restaurant for a birthday brunch, and while the place looked gorgeous from the outside and the ambiance inside was genuinely beautiful, the experience unfortunately went downhill from there.

We arrived around 2pm and were surprised to find ourselves as the only group in the entire restaurant. Even by the time we left, only a few more tables had filled, which felt unusual for the area.

The music was lovely, but the service was extremely disappointing. We asked about the mimosa special, and instead of simply explaining it, our waitress pushed us toward the meal‑mimosa combo. Once we finally ordered, getting refills felt like begging. The staff spent most of their time sitting around and admiring themselves rather than checking on tables or providing attentive service.

The food was also a miss for me, not only underwhelming, but it actually made me feel ill later that night. It’s such a shame, because the space has so much potential, but the overall vibe just didn’t match the aesthetic.

On top of that, the tables wobbled, the bathrooms had no paper towels, and the wallpaper was peeling , small details, but they add up quickly, especially when there are plenty of other upscale brunch spots nearby that offer better service, better food, and a cleaner environment.

Sadly, I wouldn’t recommend this brunch spot. Beautiful setting, but the experience didn’t live up to it.

Wow! Awesome place on Sand lake named Mamazzita , happy hour is from 6pm- 8pm and it’s BOGO!!! Food is great and drinks as well!

reviews.text:
I initially contacted Winter Park Garage Door Repair to fix my garage door and spoke with the owner, who was polite at first. He scheduled someone to come out and give me a quote. I was told the door needed to be replaced. After consulting with my mom, who was quoted $2,300, we were later told it would cost an additional $800 for windows.

My mom requested a detailed, line-by-line breakdown to understand the extra cost, even though the total price had already been agreed upon. The owner’s response was extremely unprofessional, he became disrespectful, refused to provide the breakdown, and even said he would charge her more “when she comes back.”

The whole experience felt childish and unprofessional, which was disappointing given how polite he seemed at the start. I wouldn’t recommend this company based on how poorly the situation was handled.

I decided to give Winter Park Garage Door Repair a try since I live in the area and like to support local businesses, plus all the 5-star reviews (which I will address below.)  Unfortunately, it is probably the last time I will call this company.   I was given a two-hour window, 1-3 pm, for the tech to arrive, so I made sure I was home.  However, instead of just showing up when promised, the tech chose to call ahead from his cell phone, an out-of-state number, “to make sure someone was home.”   I saw an out of state call come in, and reasonably assumed it was one of the dozen or more spam calls I get every day, so as usual I let it go to voicemail, assuming the caller would identify himself and leave a message if it was legit.   Instead, he just hung up, then called again a few more times, but each time, just hung up, no message.   At that point, instead of just proceeding to my home and ringing the doorbell, he brushed me off and went on to the next appointment.  After multiple calls back and forth to the company during my promised service window, he finally arrived, but by then it was well after 5 pm.   He proceeded to explain that he calls ahead to avoid showing up to find nobody home.   Message to company:  your tech needs to show up for his scheduled appointments, period.  If it happens to be an inconsiderate homeowner who is not home when he arrives, it’s called the cost of doing business… your problem, not mine.  Meanwhile, conscientious homeowners such as myself have their time wasted and you lose a good, potentially long-term client.  I tried to explain all this to the tech, but I have no reason to believe he really “gets it,” and won’t continue to do the same with other customers.  Also, regarding the company's 80-plus Google reviews, I do find it very "fishy" that every single review, except two, are 5-star.   Meanwhile regarding the repair… about the same time the tech finally arrived, I  actually managed to identify the issue with the door and get it corrected myself, so, after all the aggravation and wasted time, no service was actually done.

What impressed me most was their ability to tackle such a wide range of tasks while keeping the project on schedule. The crew was respectful of my home, cleaned up after themselves, and communicated clearly throughout the process.

If you’re looking for an electrical company that delivers quality workmanship and peace of mind, Sumatime LLC Electric is the one to call. I highly recommend them and will definitely use them again for future projects.
I had 3 outlets added to my home and the job done was very clean and quick. I highly recommend using this company for all of your electrical needs

recently hired Andrew to mount my TV, and I couldn’t be happier with the results! From start to finish, the experience was fantastic. He arrived on time, came fully prepared, and clearly knew exactly what he was doing. The installation was done quickly, safely, and with incredible attention to detail.
He made sure the TV was perfectly centered and securely mounted, hid all the wires neatly, and even took the time to explain everything before leaving. Professional, friendly, and incredibly skilled — it’s rare to find someone who takes such pride in their work.
I highly recommend Andrew to anyone needing electrical work or TV mounting. I’ll definitely be calling him again for future projects!
Would highly recommend. Having someone who’s going to show up and get the job done right the first time is hard to come across nowadays. The owner never disappoints.
I had Sumatime install an exhaust fan in my shower. Andrew was super professional, explained everything he would do with clear pricing, worked fast, and kept the space clean. Awesome stuff.

name: Kissimmee Heating & Cooling
address: 728 US-92, Kissimmee, FL 34741, USA

reviews.text: Our service guy was fantastic. We asked him to come replace our old furnace in the house we bought a few months ago. He came and assessed the situation with the furnace and gave us a detailed quote on different furnace options. We were very impressed with his professionalism. If we have any future heating or cooling needs, will definitely contact Paramount Heating & Air Conditioning.

I have used Paramount Heating & Air Conditioning for over 10 years. They are always professional and courteous, and they always make sure that my needs are taken care of. I highly recommend them to anyone looking for a reliable heating and air conditioning company!..

Paramount Heating & Air Conditioning is trustworthy and straightforward. They presented the options to either fix or replace my almost 30 year old HVAC system and were extremely helpful in answering questions to help us decide next steps. We opted to fix the current unit and will not hesitate to call Paramount Heating & Air Conditioning when the time comes to purchase a new HVAC system.

I was very happy with Paramount Heating & Air Conditioning. The technician was very professional and knowledgeable on what he was doing. He made sure that my home was comfortable before leaving.

I would recommend this company to anyone who needs heating or air conditioning services in the future...

After 15 frustrating minutes trying to get an appointment scheduled using a certain contractor, I hung up and turned to Google to find a heating repair company. Paramount Heating & Air Conditioning got very good reviews and they did not disappoint. Waking up to no heat in the house was not good but after a call to Paul, they arrived in 30 minutes and quickly fixed the problem. I recommend this company.

name: Jojoto grill
address: 507 W Vine St, Kissimmee, FL 34741, USA

reviews.text:
10/10
The Picanha was really good. Everything freshly made. Restaurant clean and welcoming environment. Highly recommend it.
Delicious! The sopa de costilla is incredible one of the best I’ve had. This is my second visit, and it’s just as good as the first!

The Santa Barbara Picanha is DELICIOUS ! It has fresh Grilled Steak, white cheese, pico de Gallo with garlic sauce. You have got to taste the Sopa de Res, with a subtle Caribbean condiments with yuca, corn, plantain and soft beef that falls off the bone !! And we had the 8 Oz. PICANHA , ohhh my goodness, it was cooked to perfection. The waitress Jessica, was happy, energetic, very attentive and very well versed on the menu. Her recommendations were excellent. Thank you Jessica, you are the Best. Jojoto is a definite favorite of ours. It was was our first visit of many to come. 🩷🙏

I moved to Orlando a year and an half ago and it’s been a struggle to find good food. But hands down Jojoto Steakhouse NEVER disappoints. I’m not even exaggerating. This is like my 100th time coming since I moved. They started as a food truck hidden behind a 711 and made their way to a nice small restaurant. I got tacos for apps-delicious and My roommate and I always share the 24oz mix that comes with Steak, Chicken, chorizo 4 sides big enough for today & lunch tomorrow with their homemade green sauces *chef kiss*. The pictures speak for itself 10/10.
I moved 20 min away from where I was previously making this commute now 30 min & it’s worth it for sure. Im upset it even took me this long to write a review.
Thank you Jojoto for always fulfilling my tastebuds.

Weekday lunch around 0100pm.

We weren't sure if it was order at the counter or table service. We ended up just picking a spot by the window. Menu was available as a qr code on the table.

Had a cheese cachapa ($13.99!), friend got one with steak, and we split some arepas with cheese.

Food was warm and tasted good. Thick cachapa, too thick for me, but lots of cheese.

Overall, not bad, but a little steep for me.

name: Titi Jessica's New Yorican Restaurant
address: 121 Broadway, Kissimmee, FL 34741, USA

reviews.text:
Guys spread the word. This is such a cute little place and the atmosphere is like home. It’s just like going to your Titi’s house to eat. The food is delicious and the prices are extremely affordable. They have dominoes, music, a beautiful aesthetic and everyone is so kind! I love this little spot. Spread the word and come visit Titi Jessica. This is definitely a gem! I am just a friendly neighbor but they treat you like you are family.

I got a bacalaito, kids beef empanadas with fries, Titi Jessica’s Mofongo with pernil, coco rico, soda, and agua de piña (so refreshing btw)

We just came here for the first time and it was soooooo good.  We met Titi Jessica and found that I have a lot in common with her.  The New Yorker in me resurfaced for a little while🤣.  I lived in Queens and the Bronx a while back and Tino was born in Brooklyn. The food was great. We will definitely be back.

I am thorn to give 4 stars because Jessica gave us a great service.  But the mofongo was flavorful but came apart i am used to the compacted mofongo.  The fried shrimp and even then tostones were very good.  The Titi sauce was really good but the habichuelas had a lot of cummin. The salad was very nice and fresh.  The place has a lot of potential and great service.

Food was excellent. New york style stuffed mofongo stuffed with pernil. The pernil was very good not dry and well seasoned. The caldo on the side was from beef stew, different but excellent. Fresh passion fruit juice very good. I will definitely be back.

What a gem. This place nails comfort food done right.
I ordered the pernil with rice, beans, and a salad, and everything was spot on. The pork was tender and flavorful, the rice perfectly cooked, and the beans rich without being heavy. Even the simple salad was fresh and appreciated—it rounded the plate out nicely.
The little touches really stood out: oil and vinegar at the table, clean presentation, and a welcoming, unpretentious vibe that makes you want to slow down and enjoy the meal. I also grabbed a hot chocolate because it was a chilly day—and wow, smooth and comforting. Exactly what I didn’t know I needed.
You can tell this is food made with care. I’ll absolutely be back.

name: Mu Restaurant
address: 3092 Michigan Ave, Kissimmee, FL 34744, USA

reviews.text:
This is a gem of place - the food and service is fantastic. Try the mufungitos!!!   The owner is a true owner and operator.

They are quiet during lunch but we loved the experience.  At night, they have music and a good nightlife.

Side note - their bathrooms are immaculate.
Come, come, come.... We love this gem!

Thanks for the experience and the Spanish lesson Leo!

This was our first time trying MU Restaurant, and it seems like it could be a great hangout spot for dinner, especially with what looked like a setup for live music or a DJ. However, our visit happened on a tough afternoon for the staff. There was only one server, Luis, who worked incredibly hard to manage five full tables, each with four or more people. Despite being short-staffed, Luis did his best to provide attentive service and carry the weight.

The food we ordered tasted good, but we felt the portions were small for the price, which seemed a bit high. Overall, this restaurant might be a better fit for adults looking to enjoy music, drinks, and a social atmosphere rather than a casual family dinner. Hopefully, our experience was just an off night, as the place does have potential.

We recently visited Mu Restaurant and had a mixed experience.

Appetizer: Pork Chicharrones ($15.45) – The pieces were very small, and the portion felt overpriced for what we received.

Main Dishes:

Parrilla Venezolana ($56.65) – The flavor was good, but the portion size did not justify the high price.

Mu Burger ($19.57) – Great taste, but it was not cooked to our request, which required waiting for a replacement. Even then, the burger was quite small.

Dessert:
The Bread Pudding was the highlight of our visit...absolutely delicious and easily the best part of the meal!

The service was excellent; the staff is incredibly kind and attentive. However, the prices felt too high for the portion sizes offered. While the flavors and service were good, the value for money left us disappointed.

I learned about this place on social media. I read a lot a good reviews so I decided to try it. The place is in a gas station which didn’t matter to me as long as the food is good. But unfortunately it didn’t meet up to what I expected. The presentation of the food was nice but the flavor not the best. I had pork chuckling (chicharrón de cerdo) and the mix platter ( parrilla venezolana) for appetizers we had the mofongitos they were good.
Someone in my party also had the burger which she didn’t find to be excellent. When we went to order dessert they only had 1 dessert and it was not the one I wanted so we opted for none. I also ordered some alcoholic drinks one was good the other very bad.

After seeing stellar reviews, we were surprised to arrive and find this restaurant attached to the back side of a gas station. To be honest, we almost left. But I'm glad we stayed because the food was excellent. We were the only people there, initially, in the early afternoon. I ordered a lunch special of chicken and rice, my son got the tuna tacos, and my boyfriend got the burger. We also got a shrimp  appetizer, and were brought complementary soup. Everything was very tasty.

Our only complaint was the bill... We were charged $99 for the meal, which we can not figure how we got there when we add up everything we had. It should have been closer to $70, ($20 burger, $15 tacos, $9 lunch special, $15 appetizer, 2 lemonades). Unless the lemonade was $30, we can't figure out how we got to $99 (pre tip). Unfortunately we were in a hurry and not paying attention, so we didn't realize it until after we left. I would suggest asking to see an itemized receipt in the future if it seems off.

name: Southern Breeze
address: 5770 W Irlo Bronson Memorial Hwy, Kissimmee, FL 34746, USA

reviews.text: Brilliant bar / restaurant sat outside watching the band. Drinks lovely. Food great

Approached by all 3 waitresses asking if we would like to order. Took a while to get our cheese steak and fries. Also ordered nachos. Nachos and fries were colder than room temperature. The cheese steak was warm but not hot. Told the waitresses and she took nachos off the bill. We didn't eat any of them except the initial bite to determine that it was cold.
Super chill vibes and the drinks are ice-cold with a killer selection. Seriously, if you're looking for a spot, this is it. 10/10 recommend.
Food was amazing I got a cheesesteak sub amd a shrimp po boy and a margarita!!! They had live music it was such a vibe!!!!

A nice open air restaurant/bar with good drinks and service despite being short staffed. The food was alright but the frozen drinks were great. It was a nice respite from the heat while exploring Old Town.

name: Miguelo's Ultra Lounge
address: 219 Broadway, Kissimmee, FL 34741, USA

reviews.text: The food was ok but the server forgot to put my order in so I had to wait longer, the Jack and coke i ordered was so weak. The music was not all that and that I felt like so many young people were at this place. The vibes were not what I was expecting at all. Its not a bad place if u just want to stay local and have a drink or two but if you are trying to turn up an have a great time dancing then this is not the place. On top of that I was charged $20 entrance fee for this wack band that was singing someone else's music lol. This place is a bit expensive as well. The bathroom is so outdated and the vents are so dusty and the stalls are hanging on for dear life, I dont know how they even pass inspection.

The place is really very good. The music, food, and service are all excellent. However, we had one unpleasant experience. After dinner, we ordered a second bucket of beer and asked the waitress if we could sit at one of the available tables in the fenced area downstairs. She said it would be fine, and there would be no problem.
When we arrived, they had even offered us a table in that same area, but at the time we preferred to sit at the bar. Unfortunately, shortly after we sat down, someone came and asked us to leave because that area is only for customers who purchase a bottle, something that had never been explained to us. We felt embarrassed having to stand up and leave after being told it was allowed. This policy should be communicated clearly to avoid situations like this.

By far the best Spanish food that I’ve had in years. So authentic, customer service was the best thank you, to our waitress Charlene. My family & I will visit this location again soon. Please check them out you will love the food & the service. Nice music also .

This place is flexible. Has a good familiar environment and late night has an awesome environment to dance, sing and enjoy between adults. The food is delicious and the waitress was always monitoring our status with the food and drink.

The food was good and for how packed it was came out really quickly.  We ordered trifongo with chicharron de pollo, chuletas and carne frita and it was all good.  We also got jugo de parcha, good too.  The only thing we didn't like was they didn't want to separate a big party table so when we got there we waited like 20min and finally they just separated it to seat us.  They have live music and salsa on Sunday night.  They have a dance floor.  Be prepared for it to be super loud.  Music was blasting.  They have a bar and hookah.

name: The Hen & Hog, Winter Park, FL
address: 221 W Fairbanks Ave, Winter Park, FL 32789, USA

reviews.text:
I met my sister and her husband, who were visiting Winter Park for a museum trip with their church group and my brother-in-law suggested we try The Hen and Hog. Some of us walked since it was just a short distance from the museum, while others drove over. It’s a cozy little spot, but the staff did a great job accommodating our large group of about 30, thanks to the arrangements we made ahead of time.

Our server, AJ, was a delight—friendly, organized, and full of energy. She had a great system for taking our orders efficiently, but the owner kindly reminded us that this is truly a made-to-order restaurant, which is rare these days. You can really tell the food is freshly prepared.

Most of our group ordered their famous smash burgers, and everyone raved about them. I had the ribs, which were tender and flavorful, served with a unique coleslaw that stood out from the usual. My sister enjoyed their huge house salad, and others at the table mentioned how good the beet and chopped salads were. The only thing I’d change is that I wish there were more fries—they were that good!

The blue cheese dressing on the salads was clearly house-made, and it got several compliments. Overall, if you’re in the mood for a delicious, freshly cooked meal—whether it’s a burger, salad, or something from the grill—The Hen and Hog is definitely worth a visit.

Been wanting to try this place for months. Just didn’t live up to the hype. Burger was mid, had no crust like a smash should and there was just nothing special about it. Chicken fingers were really just okay. The fry was really shallow to it had no crunch at all and was like grilled chicken with all the calories of a fried chicken. Fries were fine. I’m a sucker for homemade sauces, and we tried 3 but only liked the ranch. Best thing we had was the Mac and cheese but even then the portion was not worth the price. Service was good and staff was kind.

This place was amazing. Such a hidden gem. The staff were so friendly and attentive. It's an all scratch so expect a little wait but it's worth the wait. Wonderful complex flavors. You can tell the chef isn't just making the food but loves making the food. The care and passion shine through. I will be a repeat customer.

P.s. Great music taste

Absolutely delicious. Rosemary honey was not something we thought we’d ever want, but it was fantastic. The sausage biscuit and egg sandwich (once combined with a jelly as it was a smidge dry) was one of the best sausage biscuit sandwiches I have ever had. The fried chicken was juicy, perfectly cooked with great seasoning. Also got some hush puppies which were great. Food so good, sitting now 2 days later and waiting on an order for a smash burger and Cuban sandwich (authentic cuban bread from la segunda central in Miami). Also, cute place and a really nice hostess, Athena, who took the time to explain their menu. Only issue I have is that I took this long to try this place. Might become one of my favorite places in Orlando.

Super-friendly staff.  Fun decor 🙂
Very non-chain vibes.

Their Cuban sandwich is excellent; particularly liked the roast pork and Gouda cheese

The fries were crispy and well-seasoned
